items.title: Potential students allege visa scam tied to education consultancy

children.content: Aspiring students looking to study abroad in Australia say they've been left blindsided and with their futures in doubt after an education consultancy promised them migration and visa advice.

gallery.altText: A graphic pictures a thoughtful woman and text reading 'Visa expiring soon? Discuss your options'.

thumbnail.altText: A graphic pictures a thoughtful woman and text reading 'Visa expiring soon? Discuss your options'.

items.title: Nude swimmers take chilly plunge in Dark Mofo solstice swim

children.content: Dark Mofo's Nude Solstice Swim marks the end of Tasmania's fortnight-long winter arts festival.

image.altText: Crowd of swimmers prepare to enter the water for Dark Mofo's 2026 Nude Solstice Swim

items.title: Mona unveils $100m underground library packed with rare literary treasures

children.content: From Shakespeare's First Folio to neon-lit shelves to a curvy kids' section, Mona's new library, Phrontisterion,
is anything but ordinary.

image.altText: Book on display with hand reaching for a dial device.

items.title: Tasmanian logs made their way to Victoria. Here's what we found when we gave chase

children.content: Four Corners follows the logs, and the money, to uncover how efforts to end logging have shifted the timber trade across state borders.

image.altText: A photo taken through a windshield of a truck with logs on it driving on a road.

gallery.altText: A photo shot through the windscreen of a car, showing a truck loaded with logs ahead on a semi-suburban street.

thumbnail.altText: A photo shot through the windscreen of a car, showing a truck loaded with logs ahead on a semi-suburban street.

items.title: British PM expected to resign after challenger's thumping by-election win

children.content: Less than two years after being elected in a landslide, the Labour PM is expected to announce his resignation to make way for another leader.

image.altText: Keir Starmer looking down slightly with a neutral expression, walking towards the camera.

items.title: Green drought like 'trying to live on watermelon' for livestock

children.content: A splash of green has returned to New South Wales paddocks that were bare and barren just weeks ago, but the soil is still depleted and so are farmers' bank accounts.

gallery.altText: Rolling green hills under a cloudy sky
Cattle eating grain with shoot of green gras coming through
A woman in a brown felt hat smiles to camera

thumbnail.altText: Rolling green hills under a cloudy sky
Cattle eating grain with shoot of green gras coming through
A woman in a brown felt hat smiles to camera

items.title: Signs of life in Ningaloo Reef's baby coral after damaging year

children.content: After suffering its worst bleaching event on record, only to be battered by Cyclone Narelle six months later, the World Heritage-listed reef is showing its first signs of recovery thanks to the efforts of marine scientists.

image.altText: Marine researchers goes underwater to place spawning cones to gather and preserve coral reefs.

items.title: Australia signs 'biggest' defence export deal with Canada for $2.5b radar

children.content: Australia has signed its biggest-ever defence export deal, with Canada purchasing surveillance radar technology in a $2.5b agreement. Follow live.

image.altText: Richard Marles radar Canada

items.title: There's more to housing demand than population growth

children.content: The problem for both the Coalition and Labor is that things are only going to get more difficult for ordinary families in the years ahead, and the economic environment is likely to keep favouring One Nation's message.

gallery.altText: A woman with orange hair and a blue suit gestures while speaking

thumbnail.altText: A woman with orange hair and a blue suit gestures while speaking

items.title: Final beanie festival a hit with crowds as $400k worth of caps sold

children.content: The 30th and final Alice Springs Beanie Festival has been a sellout, setting a new record of $400,000 worth of beanies sold in three days.

image.altText: A woman with short brown bob and red shirt, wearing a pink beanie which features two large black cockatoos on top of it.

items.title: Jacob Elordi, Robert Irwin, Lisa Millar and more snag 2026 Logie nominations

children.content: The annual awards show also introduced a new category honouring Australian sports journalists, named after legendary commentator Bruce McAvaney.

image.altText: A fresh-faced woman holds four border collies puppies in her arms as she beams with glee

items.title: History quiz: Where was Ned Kelly finally captured?

children.content: Can you answer our questions about Anna Wintour, Australian tennis royalty and the doughnuts of the past? Test yourself.

gallery.altText: A large statue of Ned Kelly in his armour holding a gun

items.title: Think writing off something on tax makes it free? It doesn't

children.content: Advertisers tout EOFY sales as a prime time for big buys, and hint at the promise of a generous tax refund, but that's not quite how it works.

image.altText: A collage of a series of end-of-financial-year sales tickets.

items.title: Auction clearance rates fall to lowest level in six years

children.content: Not since the early days of the pandemic have sellers seen such disappointing results at auction, with Cotality predicting "a further loss of momentum" in the housing market.

image.altText: A man wearing a suit gesticulates at an auction with a house behind

items.title: Why super will be paid at the same time as wages from next week

children.content: Many businesses support the payday superannuation changes, but some are concerned they will not be able to meet the obligations.

gallery.altText: Woman with blonde hair and blue shirt sits at desk looking at camera

thumbnail.altText: Woman with blonde hair and blue shirt sits at desk looking at camera

items.title: This dog's cancer treatment 'excited' the US health boss but humans face hurdles

children.content: Rosie the dog had her tumours treated by an mRNA vaccine developed with the help of artificial intelligence. Her owner had decided to explore treatment options using AI chatbots.

image.altText: Paul and Rosie in the backyard

items.title: Clark avoids mammoth collapse to win US Open

children.content: Wyndham Clark holds his nerve to claim a one-stroke victory, as he lifts the US Open trophy for the second time in his career.

image.altText: Wyndham Clark celebrates with caddie David Pelekoudas after winning US Open.

items.title: Cocaine bricks wash up on Fiji beach as 'suspicious yachts' sail by

children.content: Twenty-seven bricks of cocaine have washed up around Fiji's remote Komo Island in recent weeks. Residents are worried about what the find will bring.

gallery.altText: a Fijian man on a beach looking out to sea

thumbnail.altText: a Fijian man on a beach looking out to sea

items.title: Gambling reform consultation slammed as 'charade' by health experts

children.content: The government is preparing to push ahead with a watered-down version of recommendations from the landmark Murphy inquiry, which called for a comprehensive ban on gambling advertising.

image.altText: Football players play in front of a sportsbet banner.

items.title: Concerns over AI and data centres a dangerous force for Labor

children.content: While the government is trying to assure people there is nothing to fear from AI, many think technology is moving too quickly for authorities to keep up.

image.altText: A man in a vampire outfit lies on the steps of an office building with protest signs against data centres behind him.

items.title: Hunters and wildlife rescuers share concerns about urban sprawl near lake

children.content: Duck hunters and wildlife rescuers question whether Geelong's hunting zone will be axed due to the influx of new residents so close to shooting.

gallery.altText: Man in camoflague wades through lake with suburban houses in the background

thumbnail.altText: Man in camoflague wades through lake with suburban houses in the background

items.title: Backlash as council compares rate rise to bread and cheese

children.content: Residents are frustrated as councils pass increasing costs to ratepayers already struggling with the cost-of-living crisis.

image.altText: A woman holding a dog, standing in front of a garden at the front of her home.

items.title: Is it possible to have a monoculture? This WA town doesn't think so

children.content: A Muslim leader from a regional WA town says he would like to have a conversation with Pauline Hanson about her ideology following her comments calling for monocultural Australia.

image.altText: Katanning Imam Alep Mydie.

items.title: How gambling companies 'conduct psychological experiments' on their customers

children.content: Betting ads on TV have been front of mind in Canberra lately, but experts say we're overlooking the real firepower behind Australia's gambling industry: cutting-edge, hyper-personalised, online tech.

gallery.altText: A photo over the shoulder of a woman as she looks at a gambling app on her phone.

thumbnail.altText: A photo over the shoulder of a woman as she looks at a gambling app on her phone.

items.title: France on 'red alert' as temps hit 40C in Europe heatwave

children.content: Half of France is set to be placed on "red alert" as temperatures climb beyond 40 degrees Celsius across mainland Europe.

image.altText: A woman holds a hand fan over her head in front of the Eiffel Tower.

items.title: Queensland watch houses overcrowded nearly a year after internal review

children.content: The Queensland Police Union says chronic overcrowding is still putting officers and prisoners at risk, despite government pledges to overhaul the system.

image.altText: Sign outside Brisbane watch house sign warning "no entry"

items.title: Delays to alcohol delivery restrictions worry SA anti-violence advocates

children.content: Family violence prevention advocates say the SA government has yet to progress alcohol delivery legislation as recommended by last year's Royal Commission into Domestic, Family and Sexual Violence.

image.altText: a close-up of someone using an iphone to purchase alcohol online

items.title: Serena Williams to make Wimbledon singles comeback

children.content: Serena Williams will make a stunning return to singles at this year's Wimbledon after accepting the final wildcard berth by the All England Club.

image.altText: Serena Williams with thw 2016 Wimbledon singles trophy.

items.title: Macadamia orchard area expands up east coast, doubling in size

children.content: The macadamia production area in Australia has more than doubled in the past decade, but the price is expected to hold strong for consumers.

gallery.altText: Workers hand-sorting macadamias on a conveyer belt.

thumbnail.altText: Workers hand-sorting macadamias on a conveyer belt.
